notes: Phase 2a adjudication converts all four ch.23 chronology claims (GC-23-008/009/010/012, dossier D01) from Phase-1 'disputed' to as-written 'improbable' rulings, so they now enter the discrepancy analysis; each is chain-inherited from the 457 BC anchor (GC-18-060) already adjudicated failing, so chapter 23 contributes no fresh independent failure of its own -- it inherits and rhetorically reinforces ('unquestionably fulfilled') the previously adjudicated dating chain. All four retain centrality 5 and load_bearing true because paragraphs 3-4 explicitly stake a universal 'all... unquestionably fulfilled' claim on this exact four-link sequence to license the chapter's pivot from an earthly to a heavenly sanctuary. Severities are set at 2-3 rather than at fabrication levels, since every weakened reading recovers to well-supported or probable, showing each as-written failure is chiefly a precision/absoluteness overclaim (frame) layered on the anchor's fact-level failure rather than an invented event. Impact scores: GC-23-008=10, GC-23-009=15, GC-23-010=10, GC-23-012=15; none is an independent failure point in its own right.

GC-23-008.reasoning: The failing component is a fact-level claim (autumn-season precision for the 457 BC decree's effective date); the independent failure point is GC-18-060, not a fresh error, since ch.23 merely restates and rhetorically hardens ('had been found,' 'unquestionably fulfilled') an anchor already adjudicated improbable. Framing_gap of 3 shows the weakened version -- Millerite chronology so dated it, following the received Ussher-margin reading -- is well-supported, so the substance survives and the as-written failure is chiefly an absoluteness/precision overclaim, not fabrication. Centrality stays 5 because this is the first link the chapter cites as proof the reckoning contains 'no error,' licensing the pivot from earthly to heavenly sanctuary; deleting it breaks the paragraph-4 universal claim that 'all' specifications were 'unquestionably fulfilled,' so it remains load-bearing for the chapter's rhetorical move even though the underlying failure belongs to GC-18-060.

GC-23-009.reasoning: Two components fail as written: a fact-level claim (Christ's baptism dated precisely to AD 27) and a frame-level claim ('exactly fulfilled the specification'), both ruled improbable, with GC-18-060/065 as the independent failure point rather than a new error introduced in this restatement. Framing_gap is only 2 because even the weakened version tops out at 'probable,' not 'well-supported' -- the computed AD 27 point sits one to two years off the modal AD 28-29 window, so some substance, not just the rhetoric of exactness, remains unrecovered. Centrality is 5 because AD 27 is one of the chapter's two named 'exact fulfilment' proof-points (with GC-23-010) used to certify the chronology error-free; removing it strips the chapter of a claimed independent corroboration for the reckoning, so it is load-bearing for the paragraph 3-4 argument.

GC-23-010.reasoning: The failing component is a fact-level claim (the crucifixion year, AD 31), which the ruling calls 'scheme-generated' rather than independently attested; the independent failure point is GC-18-060/071 and this ch.23 clause duplicates that finding rather than re-deriving it. Framing_gap of 3 indicates the weakened version -- a spring Passover crucifixion somewhere in AD 30-33 after a two-plus-year ministry -- is well-supported, so the underlying event and season are solid and the as-written failure is chiefly the false precision of naming AD 31 specifically. Centrality is 5 because the chapter calls this the 'unmistakable fulfilment' anchoring the seventieth-week midpoint; it is load-bearing for the paragraph's claim that 'all the preceding specifications... had been unquestionably fulfilled,' the premise the chapter needs to justify reinterpreting the sanctuary rather than abandoning the date.

GC-23-012.reasoning: Two components fail as written: a fact-level claim (a programmatic Gentile turn dated specifically to AD 34) and a frame-level claim (a punctual, national 'sealing' of Jewish rejection at that date), both ruled improbable against Dunn-line parting-of-the-ways scholarship and the documented gradualism of the Gentile mission (Cornelius, Antioch); the independent failure point remains GC-18-060, since AD 34 is arithmetically generated from the 457 BC anchor rather than independently re-derived here. Framing_gap of 3 shows the weakened version -- persecution scattering believers in the mid-30s, with the mission extending to Gentiles progressively over the following decade -- is well-supported, so the as-written failure is a punctual-event/programmatic-pivot overclaim layered on the anchor's fact failure, not an invented episode. Centrality is 5 because AD 34 is the terminus the chapter uses to launch the '1810 years remain... to 1844' arithmetic; deleting it removes the last named historical peg before the leap to 1844, so it is load-bearing for the specific chain this paragraph builds toward the chapter's investigative-judgment conclusion.
